project.name: Dictation Mate 2.0
project.description: AI-powered English learning platform with topic-based content, virtual study rooms, and AI-powered practice modes.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Environment configuration template defining GEMINI_API_KEY and APP_URL variables for the application.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project documentation covering run and deploy instructions for the AI Studio app.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry point that loads the React application via /src/main.tsx script tag.

nodes.name: metadata.json
nodes.summary: Project metadata file containing name, description, and frame permissions configuration.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js project configuration defining dependencies including React, Vite, TailwindCSS, Express, and Google GenAI SDK.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Main React application component that defines the app structure with sidebar, bottom navigation, and routing to all pages.

nodes.name: main.tsx

nodes.summary: Core TypeScript type definitions including Page, CEFRLevel, Video, Topic, YouTubeInfo, and practice-related interfaces.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Application constants including TOPICS array and MOCK_VIDEOS sample data for development.

nodes.name: index.css

nodes.name: mockLibrary.ts
nodes.summary: Mock data library providing sample folder structures and user videos for testing and development.

nodes.name: youtube.ts
nodes.summary: YouTube URL validation, video ID extraction, and metadata fetching utilities.

nodes.summary: Validates if a given string is a valid YouTube URL pattern.

nodes.name: extractVideoId
nodes.summary: Extracts the video ID from a YouTube URL string.

nodes.name: fetchYouTubeInfo
nodes.summary: Fetches YouTube video metadata and information from a URL.

nodes.summary: React custom hook for managing practice session layout state including panel configurations.

nodes.summary: Custom React hook that provides practice layout state management for resizable panels.

nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration with ES2020 target, JSX support, and path mapping for module resolution.

nodes.summary: Vite build configuration with React plugin and TailwindCSS integration.

nodes.summary: AI-generated summary panel displaying video overview, key vocabulary with IPA pronunciation, useful patterns, grammar points, and practice suggestions.

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Build system, TypeScript compiler settings, package dependencies, and environment templates that define the project structure and build pipeline

layers.name: Domain Layer
layers.description: Core TypeScript type definitions, application constants, mock data fixtures, and project documentation that define the data models and business domain

layers.name: Utility Layer
layers.description: Shared helper functions like YouTube URL validation and global CSS styles used across the application

layers.name: Hooks Layer
layers.description: React custom hooks for reusable stateful logic, including practice layout management

layers.name: UI Components Layer
layers.description: React components including navigation bars, page-level views (Home, Explore, Practice, Profile), and feature-specific subcomponents for video creation, library management, and practice modes

layers.name: Application Entry
layers.description: Application bootstrap and root component that wires together all layers and renders the React application

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with README.md to understand the project's purpose as an AI-powered English learning platform. The documentation outlines the setup requirements including Node.js and Gemini API key configuration, providing context for how the application is structured and deployed.

tour.title: Application Bootstrap
tour.description: The main.tsx file is the application entry point that bootstraps the React application. It mounts the root component to the DOM and sets up the foundation for the component tree. This is where the React app comes to life.
tour.languageLesson: React applications typically render a root component into a DOM node using createRoot(). The HTML file provides the mount point (usually a div with id 'root'), and main.tsx handles the React initialization.

tour.title: Root Component & Routing
tour.description: App.tsx serves as the root component with the highest fan-out in the codebase, importing and orchestrating all major page components. It defines the application's routing structure and layout, importing the core types and navigation components that every page relies on.

tour.title: Domain Types & Constants
tour.description: The types.ts file has the highest fan-in, meaning it's the most widely depended upon module. These TypeScript definitions establish the shared vocabulary used across all components. The constants file complements types with application-wide configuration values.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript type definitions at the domain layer create a contract that all components must respect. Centralizing types prevents drift between implementations and enables IDE autocomplete across the entire codebase.

tour.title: Global Styling
tour.description: The index.css file provides global CSS styles that establish the visual foundation. These styles apply across all pages and components, defining the design system's base variables, typography, and layout primitives that child components inherit.

tour.title: Custom Hooks & Utilities
tour.description: Custom hooks like usePracticeLayout.ts encapsulate reusable React logic, keeping components clean and focused on presentation. The youtube.ts utility provides domain-specific helper functions for YouTube URL handling, centralizing external service integration.
tour.languageLesson: Custom React hooks follow the 'use' naming convention and can call other hooks. They allow stateful logic to be extracted from components and shared across the application, following the DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle.

tour.title: Core Page Components
tour.description: HomePage.tsx and ExplorePage.tsx represent the primary user-facing surfaces. Home serves as the dashboard where users begin their learning journey, while Explore enables discovery of new content. These pages consume the domain types and integrate with the navigation system from Step 3.

tour.title: Practice Modes
tour.description: PracticePage.tsx is the centerpiece of the application where dictation, shadowing, and speaking practice happens. It orchestrates the three practice panels (Dictation, Shadowing, Speaking) that implement the core English learning methodology through interactive exercises.

tour.title: Content Management
tour.description: The video management workflow includes AddVideoPage.tsx and MyVideosPage.tsx for content creation and library management. Supporting components like AddVideoForm.tsx and AddVideoModal.tsx handle the UI for adding new learning materials, while library components (FolderSidebar, LibraryVideoCard) organize the user's collection.

tour.title: Social & Gamification
tour.description: VocabPage.tsx, StudyRoom.tsx, and MasteryLab.tsx provide specialized learning environments. The gamification layer includes LeaderboardPage.tsx for competitive motivation and StatisticsPage.tsx for progress tracking. ProfilePage.tsx rounds out the user experience with personalization settings.
